w20: Accelerator-optimized instance featuring one NVIDIA Tesla A100 GPU and 85GB RAM for high-performance machine learning and AI inference tasks.
c150: Google Cloud Platform a2-highgpu-1g: Accelerator-optimized server featuring 1 NVIDIA Tesla A100 GPU, 12 vCPUs, and 85GB RAM for specialized compute tasks.
c200: The Google Cloud Platform a2-highgpu-1g is an accelerator-optimized instance equipped with 1 NVIDIA Tesla A100 GPU, 12 vCPUs, and 85GB of RAM, designed for demanding machine learning and AI inference workloads.
w150: The Google Cloud Platform a2-highgpu-1g is an accelerator-optimized instance built on x86_64 architecture. It features 12 dedicated vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon processor running at 2.2 GHz, supported by 85 GB of RAM. The system includes one NVIDIA Tesla A100 GPU from the Ampere family, providing 40 GB of VRAM for intensive processing tasks. No local storage is bundled with this configuration. Benchmark data indicates average multi-core CPU performance and memory bandwidth, while LLM inference speeds for small and medium models rank in the top tier. Single-core performance and specific text processing tasks show weaker results. The instance supports AVX, AVX512F, and AES instruction sets, alongside hypervisor-based virtualization. With a starting on-demand price of 0.7395 USD per hour, this server is engineered for machine learning, AI model training, and high-performance inference workloads requiring dedicated GPU acceleration.
